mnemonic: Surgeon Cuts Liver Parts, Surgeons Verify Sews
explanation: Segment I (Caudate), II, III (Left lateral), IV (Left medial), V, VI, VII (Right posterior), VIII (Right anterior). Remembered as S-C-L-P-S-V-S.
source: Couinaud C. Le Foie. Masson, 1957 | Gray's Anatomy 41st ed.

explanation: الشرائح من I إلى VIII تُقسّم الكبد وظيفياً بناءً على التوزيع الوريدي البابي. كل شريحة مستقلة جراحياً — I: الفص المذنب، II–III: الفص الجانبي الأيسر، IV: الفص الإنسي الأيسر، V–VIII: الفص الأيمن.
source: Gray's Anatomy 41st ed. | Moore's Clinically Oriented Anatomy 9th ed.

mnemonic: BD PA — Bile Duct, Portal vein, hepatic Artery
explanation: Each portal tract contains: (B)ile duct, (P)ortal vein branch, hepatic (A)rtery branch. The bile duct and hepatic artery are always smaller than the portal vein. On US, the portal triad appears as a 'Mickey Mouse sign'.
source: Netter's Atlas of Human Anatomy 8th ed. | Gray's Anatomy 41st ed.

mnemonic: بي صح — بي (قناة صفراء)، (وريد بابي)، صح (شريان كبدي)
explanation: كل ثلاثية بابية = قناة صفراء + وريد بابي + شريان كبدي. بالموجات الفوق صوتية تُشبه علامة 'ميكي ماوس': الوريد البابي الكبير بالأسفل (أذن ميكي الكبيرة)، والقناة الصفراء والشريان فوقه.
source: Gray's Anatomy 41st ed. | Rumack's Diagnostic Ultrasound

explanation: The spleen is 1 inch thick, 3 inches wide, 5 inches long. It weighs 7 oz. It lies beneath ribs 9–11. It is NOT normally palpable. Source: Moore's Clinically Oriented Anatomy — classic teaching aid.
source: Moore's Clinically Oriented Anatomy 9th ed. | Gray's Anatomy 41st ed.

explanation: الطحال بُعده 1 بوصة سمكاً، 3 بوصات عرضاً، 5 بوصات طولاً، وزنه 7 أونصات، يقع تحت الضلوع 9 إلى 11. وهو لا يُجسّ في الحالة الطبيعية.
Right kidney anterior relations (superior to inferior): (D)uodenum (2nd part), (L)iver (right lobe), (C)olon (hepatic flexure). The right kidney is lower than the left due to the liver above it.
source: Gray's Anatomy 41st ed. | Moore's Clinically Oriented Anatomy 9th ed.

mnemonic: Stomach, Spleen, Pancreas, Colon — SSPC Left
explanation: Left kidney anterior relations: (S)tomach, (S)pleen, (P)ancreas (tail), (C)olon (splenic flexure). The left kidney is higher than the right by 1–2 cm.
source: Gray's Anatomy 41st ed. | Netter's Atlas 8th ed.

mnemonic: HEAD NEXT to the Body TAILgates — HNBT
explanation: Pancreas parts: (H)ead (in duodenal C-loop), (N)eck (anterior to portal vein), (B)ody (crosses L2 vertebra), (T)ail (reaches splenic hilum). The uncinate process hooks posteriorly from the head beneath the SMA.
source: Gray's Anatomy 41st ed. | Netter's Atlas 8th ed.

explanation: أجزاء البنكرياس: الرأس (داخل حرف C الاثنا عشر)، العنق (أمام الوريد البابي)، الجسم (يعبر الفقرة L2)، الذيل (يصل إلى نقير الطحال). الناتئ الأنسي يمتد خلف الشريان المساريقي العلوي SMA.

mnemonic: MF Sub Ser — Mucosa, (no submucosa), Fibromuscular, Subserosal, Serosa
explanation: Gallbladder wall layers: Mucosa (columnar epithelium with Rokitansky-Aschoff sinuses), Fibromuscular layer, Subserosal layer (perimuscular), Serosa. Notably LACKS a submucosa and muscularis mucosa — unlike most GI tract. Normal wall ≤3 mm on fasting US.
source: Gray's Anatomy 41st ed. | Rumack's Diagnostic Ultrasound 5th ed.

mnemonic: MHV = 4 and 5/8. RHV = 5/8 and 6/7. LHV = 2 and 3/4.
explanation: Middle Hepatic Vein divides segments 4 (left) from 5 & 8 (right). Right Hepatic Vein divides segments 5/8 (anterior) from 6/7 (posterior). Left Hepatic Vein divides segments 2 (posterior) from 3/4 (anterior). Segment 1 (caudate) drains directly into IVC.
source: Couinaud C. 1957 | CT & MRI of the Whole Body, Haaga 6th ed.
